error_signature: %error: <file>:<n>: syntax error, unexpected generate <n> | generate | ^~~~~~~~ %error: <file>:<n>: syntax error, unexpected '[', expecting identifier or randomize <n> | for (p = <n>; p < pipe_depth; p = p + <n>) pipe_reg[p] <= pipe_reg[p-<n>]; | ^ %error: <file>:<n>: syntax error, unexpected endmodule <n> | endmodule | ^~~~~~~~~ %error: cannot continue
raw_error_example:
%Error: output/direct_4.sv:89:13: syntax error, unexpected generate
   89 |             generate
      |             ^~~~~~~~
%Error: output/direct_4.sv:95:76: syntax error, unexpected '[', expecting IDENTIFIER or randomize
   95 |                             for (p = 1; p < PIPE_DEPTH; p = p + 1) pipe_reg[p] <= pipe_reg[p-1];
      |                                                                            ^
%Error: output/direct_4.sv:147:1: syntax error, unexpected endmodule
  147 | endmodule
      | ^~~~~~~~~
%Error: Cannot continue
root_cause: The verilog generate block was used incorrectly for conditional pipeline instantiation, but the loop variable "p" was not declared as an integer.
fix_rule: Declare all loop variables (integer i, k, p) before use. Replace generate/endgenerate with an always_ff block and conditional if statement for pipeline stages.
